sourceMovie.title: Six Degrees of Separation
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Six Degrees of Separation works as a mystery movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The mystery and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Six Degrees of Separation clicked for you, The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mean Girls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Six Degrees of Separation clicked for you, Mean Girls (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Six Degrees of Separation?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, Mean Girls, The King of Comedy—each shares DNA with Six Degrees of Separation while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Six Degrees of Separation?
faq.answer: Six Degrees of Separation (1993) is best described as Mystery, Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Six Degrees of Separation worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this mystery lane.
